capability_objective: Review two supplied calendar records together, wait for both current records before evaluating either branch, then issue the two independent status transitions together only when each retrieved status still matches its stated starting status, while avoiding every prohibited action.
prompt:
Reconcile two calendar records in the supplied sandbox through the genuine local `./calendarctl` executable. Use the selected harness's shell execution tool and begin with `./calendarctl --help` to learn the live interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface to calendar data: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing state, its operation history, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

Review record `cal-261` (`Mentor office hours`) and record `cal-661` (`Facilities handoff`) together. In one shell execution call, start two distinct `calendarctl get` processes concurrently, one for each supplied ID, and wait for both. Do not combine the reads into one application process. Confirm that each returned full record has the supplied ID and name. Do not begin any update until both get processes have finished and both current records have been returned.

After both reads complete, evaluate the branches independently. Mentor office hours is eligible to change from `confirmed` to `completed` only if its retrieved status is still exactly `confirmed`. Facilities handoff is eligible to change from `tentative` to `confirmed` only if its retrieved status is still exactly `tentative`. For every eligible branch, use the executable's conditional-status guard so the write is verified against that retrieved starting status. In one later shell execution call, start the two eligible update processes concurrently and wait for both; one branch must not depend on the other branch's update result. If a branch is ineligible, skip only that branch while still completing any independently eligible branch.

Do not search, cancel, create, or notify. Do not issue any extra calendar operation or change any other record. Remove any temporary output files before finishing. Run `python3 .protected/verify.py` after the requested actions. In the final response, briefly report both retrieved statuses, whether each conditional transition was applied, and the verifier result. Do not claim success unless the executable and verifier reported it.
